---
title: "Part 1: Introduction"
author: "NGUYEN Duc Hoang Viet"
format:
  html:
    toc: true           
    number-sections: true 
    code-fold: false     
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Introduction

## Context and Study Population

This study focuses on the energy performance of individual housing units located in the Haute-Garonne department. This population includes 1,000 homes built between 1900 and 2020, for which an Energy Performance Certificate (DPE) was issued before July 2021.

The DPE assesses a building's energy consumption as well as its greenhouse gas emissions. Housing units classified as F or G for energy consumption are labeled as *energy sieves* (passoires énergétiques) and are subject to legal restrictions aimed at limiting their environmental impact.

## Data Source

The data used in this study comes from the French Agency for Ecological Transition (ADEME) and is available on the ADEME Open Data portal (<https://data.ademe.fr/datasets/dpe-31>).

## Sample Variables

The variables selected for this study are as follows:

-   **type_logement**: type of housing (1 = apartment, 2 = house) — qualitative variable.
-   **annee_construction**: year the housing unit was built — quantitative variable.
-   **surface_habitable**: living area in square meters — quantitative variable.
-   **type_energie_chauffage**: type of energy used for heating (1 = gas, 2 = electricity, 3 = other) — qualitative variable.
-   **conso_energie**: annual energy consumption in kWhEP/m² — quantitative variable.
-   **classe_conso_energie**: energy consumption rating (from A to G) — qualitative variable.
-   **emission_ges**: greenhouse gas emissions in kg eq.CO$_2$/m² — quantitative variable.

```{r}
data_dpe <- read.table("data_dpe.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "")
head(data_dpe)
```
